title: Repair a Small Disagreement
summary: Handle a disagreement without making it worse.
learnerPromise: Handle a disagreement without making it worse.
whyItMatters: Disagreements happen. Handling them well keeps relationships strong and shows maturity.
realLifeUses: Settle a dispute with a roommate about chores.
Resolve a group project conflict at school.
Talk through a misunderstanding with a friend.
Negotiate a fair solution with a coworker.
youWillLearn: Stay calm and listen to the other person.
miniChallenge: Think of a recent small conflict and write down what you could have said differently.
steps: Take a breath and stay calm. Do not raise your voice.
Listen to the other person fully before you respond.
Use 'I' statements like 'I felt upset when...' instead of 'You always...'
Look for something you both agree on.
Suggest a specific next step or compromise.
completionCriteria: You agree on at least one action going forward.
commonProblems: Getting emotional and raising your voice.
Trying to win instead of finding a solution.
tips: If emotions are high, take a break and come back later.
Focus on the problem, not the person.
It is okay to agree to disagree on some things.
